title: Social Security Disability Insurance — CBPP Policy Basics
description: Center on Budget and Policy Priorities (CBPP) 'Policy Basics' overview of Social Security Disability Insurance (SSDI), explaining that SSDI provides earned benefits to workers who can no longer support themselves through work due to severe, long-lasting impairments. Covers how SSDI fits within Social Security and is administered by SSA; financing via the SSDI payroll tax and trust fund; the stringent three-part eligibility criteria (insured status, severe medically determinable impairment, inability to do substantial gainful work); the demographic profile of beneficiaries; the role of each state's Disability Determination Services (DDS) in medical/vocational evaluation and the fact that fewer than 1 in 3 applicants are awarded benefits; modest benefit levels and Medicare eligibility; built-in work incentives (trial work period, earnings thresholds); and trends in SSDI enrollment since 2010. Provides authoritative plain-language background useful for contextualizing DDS disability-determination work.
